heading: Grants for regulatory barrier removal strategies and implementation
label: 12705c Grants for regulatory barrier removal strategies and implementation

children.heading: Funding
children.label: a Funding
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated for grants under subsections (b) and (c) such sums as may be necessary for each of fiscal years 2001, 2002, 2003, 2004, and 2005.1See References in Text note below.1
identifying, assessing, and monitoring State and local regulatory barriers;
identifying State and local policies (including laws and regulations) that permit or encourage regulatory barriers;
developing legislation to provide State, local, or regional programs to reduce regulatory barriers and developing a strategy for adoption of such legislation;
developing model State or local standards and ordinances to reduce regulatory barriers and assisting in the adoption and use of the standards and ordinances;
carrying out the simplification and consolidation of administrative procedures and processes constituting regulatory barriers to affordable housing, including the issuance of permits; and

providing technical assistance and information to units of general local government for implementation of legislative and administrative reform programs to remove regulatory barriers to affordable housing.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 106–569, title I, § 102(c)114 Stat. 2947Dec. 27, 2000
children.label: c Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 106–569, title I, § 102(c)114 Stat. 2947Dec. 27, 2000

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: d Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this section, the terms “regulatory barriers to affordable housing” and “regulatory barriers” have the meaning given such terms in .section 12705b of this title

children.heading: Application and selection
children.label: e Application and selection
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the form and manner of applications for grants under this section, which shall describe how grant amounts will assist the State or unit of general local government in developing and implementing strategies to remove regulatory barriers to affordable housing. The Secretary shall establish criteria for approval of applications under this subsection and such criteria shall require that grant amounts be used in a manner consistent with the strategy contained in the comprehensive housing affordability strategy for the jurisdiction pursuant to .section 12705(b)(4) of this title

children.heading: Selection of grantees
children.label: f Selection of grantees
children.text: To the extent amounts are made available to carry out this section, the Secretary shall provide grants on a competitive basis to eligible grantees based on the proposed uses of such amounts, as provided in applications under subsection (e).

children.heading: Coordination with clearinghouse
children.label: g Coordination with clearinghouse
children.text: Each State and unit of general local government receiving a grant under this section, shall consult, coordinate, and exchange information with the clearinghouse established under .section 12705d of this title

children.heading: Reports to Secretary
children.label: h Reports to Secretary
children.text: Each State and unit of general local government receiving a grant under this section shall submit a report to the Secretary, not less than 12 months after receiving the grant, describing any activities carried out with the grant amounts. The report shall contain an assessment of the impact of any regulatory barriers identified by the grantee on the housing patterns of minorities.
